title: Hit by an Uninsured Driver in Fort Worth, Texas (2026)

Texas law provides several recovery paths that many accident victims are completely unaware of, including your own Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist (UM/UIM) coverage, which you may have paid for without ever realizing it applies here.

facebook:
Finding out the driver who just hit you has no insurance is a gut punch. You did everything right — and now you're staring at car damage, possible injuries, and no clear path forward. We hear this story too often, and we want you to know: you are not out of options.

In Texas, your own auto insurance policy may include Uninsured Motorist coverage that could help cover your damages — even though the other driver caused the crash. There are also legal strategies an experienced attorney can explore on your behalf, but time matters. Texas gives you only 2 years from the date of your accident to file a claim.

If you were hit by an uninsured driver in Fort Worth, the most important thing you can do right now is get informed and get connected with someone who can protect your rights.

reddit_title: Hit by an uninsured driver in Fort Worth, TX — here's what your actual options are (UM/UIM, deadlines, and more)
reddit_body:
Getting hit by an uninsured driver in Texas feels like a dead end, but there are real recovery options worth knowing about. First, check your own auto insurance policy for Uninsured Motorist (UM) or Underinsured Motorist (UIM) coverage — many Texas drivers have it without realizing it applies in exactly this situation. Your own insurer can be required to step in and cover damages the at-fault driver cannot pay.

Second, the legal deadline matters more than most people realize. Texas has a 2-year statute of limitations for car accident claims, starting from the date of the crash. Missing that window typically means losing your right to any compensation, so even if you're still dealing with medical bills or repairs, don't let time slip by without at least consulting an attorney.

If you're not sure where to start with finding a lawyer who handles uninsured driver cases specifically, wreckmatch.com is a free tool that matches accident victims with pre-screened personal injury attorneys based on the details of their case — no cold-calling random firms or guessing who handles this type of claim.
